title: Daily Briefing — Thursday, May 21
tldr: Andrej Karpathy joined Anthropic to rebuild its pretraining research team — arguably the highest-profile AI talent move of the year.
Anthropic was closing a ~$30B round at a $900B+ valuation, as execs across Google/OpenAI/Anthropic call the frontier race “neck-and-neck.”
The strategic frame (Axios): Google's plan to win leans on cheap, fast models deployed across products used by billions.

blocks.p: Andrej Karpathy — OpenAI co-founder, former Tesla Autopilot lead, and the field's most beloved educator — joined Anthropic to rebuild its pretraining research team from the inside.
blocks.why: Pretraining talent is the scarcest resource at the frontier; where it concentrates signals who leads next.
blocks.doIt: Follow what Karpathy publishes and teaches — his explanations are among the fastest ways to level up your own mental models.

blocks.p: Anthropic was reported closing a ~$30B round at a $900B+ valuation (figures varied by source and moved fast through the month — treat as directional). Leaders increasingly call the frontier effectively neck-and-neck, differing mainly on cost, speed, and compute tradeoffs.
Axios framed Google's strategy as staying at the frontier while prioritizing models cheap and fast enough to ship to billions of users — distribution as the moat.

sources: Axios · CNBC · The Information · llm-stats.com (valuations reported same-day; treat as directional).
